title: Forest of Dean cycling & Beauchamp (John's cello course)

main_route.original_wording: M50 turn-off to Ross, on the M5
main_route.excerpt: We left at 8.30 + breakfasted at the M50 turn-off to Ross, on the M5.

main_route.normalized_name: Forest of Dean campsite, Gloucestershire, UK
main_route.original_wording: a campsite next to the Forest of Dean
main_route.excerpt: We reached a campsite next to the Forest of Dean but, disappointingly, none of them provide cycling out into the forest.

main_route.normalized_name: Beauchamp music course venue, Forest of Dean area, UK
main_route.excerpt: We reached Beauchamp at 9.45. for John's 'cello course.
Simon & Thomas left at 7.10 for Birmingham, without breakfast.

excursions.label: Cycling at Pedalaway, Forest of Dean

stops.normalized_name: Pedalaway Cycle Centre, Forest of Dean, UK
stops.excerpt: So we have driven to the Park at Pedalaway. The boys cycled before lunch.

unresolved.issue: The exact location of the 'Beauchamp' cello/strings course venue is uncertain. Based on previous family trips using the same name (1992, 1993, 1994, 1996), this is a recurring residential music course venue near or in the Forest of Dean/Gloucestershire area. Could be a school, country house or estate. Treated as approximate.
unresolved.excerpt: We reached Beauchamp at 9.45. for John's 'cello course.

boundary_flags.note: Page A_0195_L begins with the end of a prior trip returning from Belgium/France via Channel Tunnel and Broadhembury (near Ashford). That return journey is NOT part of this trip. This trip begins with the Saturday 5 April 1997 narrative partway through the same page.

summary: An Easter short break (5–8 April 1997) from Birmingham to the Forest of Dean for cycling at Pedalaway, followed by a two-night stay at the Beauchamp residential cello course venue for John. Simon drove home on Sunday evening for church duties and returned on Monday; he and Thomas made an early return to Birmingham on Tuesday morning while the rest of the family stayed for John's concert before driving home on Tuesday afternoon.
extraction_notes: Page A_0195_L is boundary-mixed: it opens with the tail end of a prior France/Belgium/Channel Tunnel trip (return through Broadhembury near Ashford, then home on Sunday via M-roads). This trip's own narrative starts partway down the same page with 'Saturday 1997 April 5th.' No crossing is required — the trip is entirely within England. The dog Tinder is noted as being in kennels. Dates 6, 7 and 8 April are stated explicitly in the diary headings (Sunday 6th April, Monday 7th April, Tuesday 8th April), so date_precision is day. Simon's return to Birmingham on Sunday (seq 5) is explicitly stated; the family's final return on Tuesday 8 April is also explicit. Beauchamp is treated as approximate and unresolved per the same convention used in 1992–1996 Beauchamp trips already in the dataset.
